_comment: ExecWrap unified configuration v1. All wrapper behavior is controlled here. See .exec/README.md for full docs.

features._comment: Master feature toggles. Set 'enabled: false' to disable a feature globally. Individual rules can override tmux and log behavior.

env_loading._comment: Load .env before every command. AI tools often skip this. Set enabled:false if you manage env another way.

adhoc_saving._comment: Capture inline scripts (bash -c '...', python -c '...', heredocs) into scripts/adhoc/ for later analysis and reuse. Files named <timestamp>_<pid>.<ext>. The path is printed in the debug banner.

logging._comment: Tee all command stdout+stderr to .log/<timestamp>_<pid>_<cmd>.log. Useful for reviewing what the AI tool did.

tmux._comment: Run commands in tmux for visibility. default_mode 'none' means tmux is OFF by default — only rules with an explicit 'tmux' field trigger it. Modes: 'window' (new window in shared session), 'session' (own session), 'none' (no tmux). Rules can override with their own 'tmux' field.

hooks._comment: Search these dirs for pre-bash/pre-command hooks and call them before execution. Hooks receive the command as $1. harden-wizard installs hooks here automatically.

hardening._comment: Multi-layer execsec hardening. Layer 1 (rules[] below) always runs. Layer 2 requires harden-wizard --with-wrapper. Layer 3 requires execsec submodule and python3+PyYAML.

guard._comment: Layer 2: execsec guard.sh — ACTIVE: installed by harden-wizard --with-wrapper. guard.sh is the check-only validator (exits 0/1, does NOT execute). guard-exec.sh alongside it is the shell replacement for direct AI tool use without execwrap.

interceptor._comment: Layer 3: intercept-enhanced.py — 57-rule YAML set covering: destructive ops, privilege escalation, system control, secret files, package management (npm/pip/yarn ask), git ops (reset --hard ask), editors (allow with logging). Requires python3 and PyYAML (pip install pyyaml). Config discovery is HARDCODED inside the interceptor — it searches (in order): .settings/permissions.yaml, .claude/permissions.yaml, .opencode/permissions.yaml, ~/.llmsec/defaults/permissions.yaml, execsec/configs/defaults/permissions.yaml. To add project-specific rules, create .settings/permissions.yaml.

rtk._comment: RTK output compression — rewrites commands through RTK for 60-90% token savings. Uses 'rtk rewrite' which handles pipes, compounds, redirects, and heredocs safely. Falls back to .exec/rtk, then PATH. Runs AFTER security layers, BEFORE execution. Disable with enabled:false or remove the rtk binary.

local_priority._comment: For each listed binary, check if ./<binary> exists in project root and prefer it. Applied BEFORE rule matching AND re-applied AFTER each rewrite. This ensures rewrite chains stack correctly: python → uv run python → ./uv run python (./uv exists in this repo). This repo has ./just and ./uv wrappers that pipe output through cat to avoid pager issues with AI tools.

debug_info._comment: Print a banner before each command showing: PID, log file path, tmux session/window, the (possibly rewritten) command, and the adhoc script file if one was saved. Set false to silence.

rules.description: Rewrite any python/python3 invocation (bare or full path) to 'uv run python'

match._comment: Matches bare 'python'/'python3' AND any path-prefixed invocation: ./.venv/bin/python, /usr/bin/python3, etc. The glob catches the exact bare word for the case-match path. Regex (\s|$) matches both 'python script.py' and bare 'python' with no args.

rewrite._comment: replace_binary strips the first word (regardless of path prefix) and substitutes 'uv run python', keeping all args. Examples: 'python script.py' → 'uv run python script.py'; './.venv/bin/python3 -c ...' → 'uv run python -c ...'. After rewrite, local_priority re-applies: 'uv' → './uv'. The full chain stacks.

rules._comment: uv detected (./uv wrapper present). After rewrite+re-priority: './uv run python script.py'. A tmux-uv-run-python-scripts rule below then fires if enabled.

rules.description: Redirect pip install to uv pip install — faster and lockfile-aware
rules._comment: uv pip install respects pyproject.toml and is significantly faster. NOTE: Layer 3 (intercept-enhanced.py) has an 'ask' rule for pip install — this rewrite runs first, so uv pip install passes through cleanly.

rules.description: Prepend 'uv run' before pytest to ensure correct venv
rules._comment: Ensures pytest runs in the project venv, not system Python. local_priority then maps uv → ./uv.

rules.description: Run named shell scripts in their own tmux window for live observation
rules._comment: Matches: bash foo.sh, bash foo.bash, zsh foo.zsh, sh foo.bsh — any named script invocation with common shell script extensions. Does NOT match bash -c '...' (inline scripts). Change tmux to 'session' for full isolation or 'none' to disable per-rule.

rules.description: Run Python scripts (via uv after rewrite) in their own tmux window
rules._comment: Fires AFTER rewrite-python-to-uv transforms 'python script.py' → './uv run python script.py'. This rule is why the rewrite chain stacks: python→uv→tmux. Must come AFTER the rewrite rules. Does NOT fire for 'python -c ...' inline scripts.

rules.description: Run justfile recipes in their own tmux window
rules._comment: Matches both 'just test' and './just test' (post-local_priority). just commands often run full pipelines — tmux visibility helps monitor them. Regex (\./)?just covers both direct and local-wrapper invocations.

rules.description: Block piping curl/wget output directly to a shell interpreter
rules.reason: Executing arbitrary remote code without inspection is a supply chain risk. The downloaded script could contain anything.
rules.alternative: Download first and inspect: curl -o /tmp/script.sh <URL> && cat /tmp/script.sh — then run manually after review.
rules._comment: Also covered by Layer 2 (guard.sh when installed). Having it in Layer 1 gives better error messages.

rules.reason: Recursive forced deletion is irreversible. Even with correct paths, typos or variable expansion bugs can wipe important data.
rules.alternative: Move to a temp location instead: mv <path> /tmp/trash-$(date +%s)/ — or use targeted deletion: find <dir> -name '*.log' -delete
rules._comment: Also in .claude/settings.json as 'Bash(rm -rf:*)'. When guard.sh (Layer 2) is installed, it adds additional exfiltration and secret-access checks.

rules.reason: Privilege escalation bypasses all security controls and can modify system-wide state.
rules.alternative: Ask the user to run the command: 'Please run with sudo: <command>'. Or restructure to work within user permissions.
rules._comment: Also caught by Layer 3 (intercept-enhanced.py). If the project legitimately needs sudo for specific operations, add a targeted allow rule ABOVE this deny rule.

rules.description: Block force-pushing to remote git repos
rules.reason: Force push rewrites shared history and can destroy collaborators' work. It also makes code review and auditing harder.
rules.alternative: Use: git push --force-with-lease (checks for concurrent upstream changes before pushing). Or discuss with the user first.
rules._comment: Layer 3 has an 'ask' rule for git push generally.

rules.description: Block uploading data to public paste/transfer services
rules.reason: Uploading to public services risks exposing secrets, credentials, or proprietary code. These services may be indexed or publicly accessible.
rules.alternative: Save output locally: command > output.txt — then share via a secure, private channel.
rules._comment: Also caught by Layer 2 (guard.sh when installed). Extend the regex if your organization has other blocked upload destinations.

rules.description: Block base64-encoding sensitive files (common exfiltration technique)
rules.reason: Base64-encoding credential files is a known exfiltration technique. Encoded data is trivially decodable.
rules.alternative: Reference credentials via environment variables. Use .env loading instead of encoding files.
rules._comment: Also caught by Layer 2 (guard.sh when installed). Add to regex if you have custom credential files.

rules.reason: chmod 777 makes files writable by any user on the system, creating privilege escalation and tampering risks.
rules.alternative: Use chmod 755 for executables (owner can write, others can read+execute) or chmod 644 for data files.

rules.description: Block bare 'uv' command — enforce use of local ./uv wrapper
rules.reason: This project uses a local ./uv wrapper that pipes output through cat to avoid pager issues with AI tools. Using system uv directly bypasses this.
rules.alternative: Use ./uv instead: ./uv run python script.py, ./uv pip install package, etc. The local_priority feature also handles this automatically.
rules._comment: Translated from .claude/settings.json deny: 'Bash(uv:*)'. local_priority (enabled above) rewrites 'uv' → './uv' before rules are checked, so this rule is a safety net for edge cases where local_priority is bypassed.

rules.description: Block bare 'just' command — enforce use of local ./just wrapper
rules.reason: This project uses a local ./just wrapper that pipes output through cat. Using system just directly may cause pager issues in AI tool environments.
rules.alternative: Use ./just instead: ./just test, ./just lint, ./just check, etc.
rules._comment: Translated from .claude/settings.json deny: 'Bash(just:*)'. local_priority rewrites 'just' → './just' automatically, so this is secondary enforcement.

rules.description: Block bare 'python' command — enforce use of uv run python
rules.reason: Bare 'python' may use system Python instead of the project virtualenv. Use 'uv run python' to ensure the correct environment.
rules.alternative: In normal flow, rewrite-python-to-uv fires FIRST and prevents this. If you see this block, re-check rule ordering.
rules._comment: Translated from .claude/settings.json deny: 'Bash(python:*)'. In normal flow, rewrite-python-to-uv (above) rewrites 'python' → 'uv run python' before reaching this rule. This is a safety net.

rules.description: Block bare 'python3' command — enforce use of uv run python
rules.reason: Bare 'python3' may use system Python instead of the project virtualenv. Use 'uv run python' to ensure the correct environment.
rules.alternative: Use: uv run python (or ./uv run python). The rewrite-python-to-uv rule handles this automatically for most cases.
rules._comment: Translated from .claude/settings.json deny: 'Bash(python3:*)'. The rewrite rule regex '^python3?(\s|$)' handles both 'python' and 'python3'.
